claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK. I'm asking because I have a child and I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know you live in the UK and have a child.

**What is your child's name and age?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex and they're 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Alex is 6 years old, so that's within the standard Child Benefit eligibility range.

**Are you living with Alex, or is Alex living with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, that's good to know. Since you live with Alex, that's one of the key requirements for eligibility.

**Are you the only person currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is someone else also claiming for them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, that's clear. Now I need to understand your residency and immigration status to ensure you meet all the eligibility requirements.

**What is your residency or immigration status in the UK? For example, are you a British citizen, do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another type of visa?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen, so I was born and raised here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for clarifying that. As a British citizen living in the UK and responsible for Alex who lives with you, you meet the key eligibility criteria.

Let me just confirm one final thing to ensure there are no circumstances that would affect your eligibility:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, residential care, or in local council care for an extended period (more than 8-12 weeks)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Alex is perfectly healthy and lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on your circumstances:
- You are a British citizen living in the UK
- You live with Alex, your 6-year-old child
- You are responsible for Alex's care and upkeep
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital or care

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex!**

To proceed, you can make a claim for Child Benefit. The next step would be to contact HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) or submit your claim through the appropriate government channels.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), so falls within the standard Child Benefit age range. You live with Alex and are responsible for their care and upkeep. You are a British citizen living in the UK, which means you have the right to reside and claim Child Benefit. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for extended periods. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. As a British citizen living in the UK, responsible for a 6-year-old child who lives with you full-time, and with no competing claims, you meet all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.">
